premises:
All runners who ranked in the top 1000 in the Boston Marathon finished the marathon within 3 hours.
All runners from the Yale Running Club ranked in the top 1000 in the Boston Marathon.
All runners wearing T-shirts with the bulldog logo are from Yale Running Club.
Some of the runners wearing T-shirts with the bulldog logo are majoring in Computer Science
Tim is a runner and if Tim finishes the Boston Marathon within 3 hours, then he is not both from Yale Running Club and ranked in the top 1000 in the Boston Marathon. 
Tim is a runner and if Tim majors in computer science, then he is either wearing a T-shirt with the bulldog logo or is a runner from the Yale Running Club.
premises-FOL:
∀x (Runner(x) ∧ RankedIn(x, top1000, bostonMarathon) → FinishWithin(x, bostonMarathon, 3Hours))
∀x (Runner(x) ∧ From(x, yaleRunningClub) → RankedIn(x, top1000, bostonMarathon))
∀x (Runner(x) ∧ Wear(x, tshirtsWithBulldogLogo) → From(x, yaleRunningClub))
∃x (Runner(x) ∧ Wear(x, tshirtsWithBulldogLogo) ∧ MajorIn(x, computerScience))
Runner(tim) ∧ (FinishWithin(x, bostonMarathon, 3Hours) → ¬(From(tim, yaleRunningClub) ∧ RankedIn(tim, top1000, bostonMarathon)))
Runner(tim) ∧ (MajorIn(tim, computerScience) → (Wear(tim, tshirtsWithBulldogLogo) ⊕ From(tim, yaleRunningClub)))
conclusion: Tim finished the Boston Marathon within 3 hours.
label: Uncertain

premises:
An optical engineer studies science and studies engineering.
Someone who studies optical engineering is an optical engineer.
An optical engineer studies the generation, transmission, or detection of light.
Murphy is an optical engineer.
premises-FOL:
∀x (OpticalEngineer(x) → Study(x, science) ∧ Study(x, engineering))
∀x (Study(x, opticalEngineering) → OpticalEngineer(x))
∀x (OpticalEngineer(x) → Study(x, lightGeneration) ∨ Study(x, lightTransmission) ∨ Study(x, lightDetection))
OpticalEngineer(murphy)
label: True

premises:
All children are human.
If someone is underage, then they are a child.
People are either underage or of age.
If someone is of age, then they can vote.
If someone is of age, they can legally get married.
If Jack is a child and a human, then Jack is neither able to vote nor get married.
premises-FOL:
∀x (Child(x) → Human(x))
∀x (Underage(x) → Child(x)) 
∀x (Underage(x) ⊕ OfAge(x))
∀x (OfAge(x) → CanVote(x))
∀x (OfAge(x) → CanGetMarried(x))
Child(jack) ∧ Human(jack) → (¬CanVote(jack) ∧ ¬CanGetMarried(jack))
label: Uncertain
